floors.summary: Monster stage for MT0 preserves the economy map and quotas while converting all combat and special-pressure candidates into allowed enemy tiles. Seven non-adjacent monsters create distinct main-route, detour, reward-guard, branch-special, and final precheck roles; the northwest pickaxe guard is locally adjusted to [1,2] so it guards the actual reward approach. Manual repair: moved northwest pickaxe guard out of [1,2]. Manual repair: moved northwest pickaxe guard into [3,3].

Monster stage for MT1: preserved the accepted topology, stairs, doors, keys, rewards, and the existing lava net. Added 9 non-adjacent allowed enemies with distinct route roles: early baseline pressure, west optional reward guard, west-loop defense tax, upper-loop attack guard, central blue-door endurance guard, central return defense gate, south shortcut tax, late east light gate, and east-spine net-route attack check. No enemy special abilities were assigned; the preserved lava net remains the concrete special-pressure source on the shorter east route. Manual repair: downgraded MT1 [8,7] yellowPotion to redPotion for potion progression.

Repaired only the monster-owned integration issue on MT2. The existing topology, stairs, tracked doors, keys, rewards, and special placements are preserved; a no-special zombie was added at [9,8] to tax the east-to-central bypass from the east yellow door before it can reach central rewards, the upper connector, or the up stair. Enemy codes remain allowed, special usage remains whitelisted with at most one special per monster, and no enemies are orthogonally adjacent. Manual repair: downgraded MT2 [1,5] yellowPotion to redPotion for potion progression. Manual repair: downgraded MT2 [2,1] bluePotion to redPotion for potion progression. Manual repair: downgraded MT2 [10,3] bluePotion to redPotion for potion progression.

Monster pass for final floor MT3: preserved the repaired economy, stairs, walls, doors, keys, gems, and potions exactly, then converted all combat and special-pressure candidates into allowed concrete enemies. Enemy codes used are 206, 211, and 214 only; redPriest 218 is intentionally unused because its current special list has two entries while this floor must enforce max_specials_per_monster=1. No enemy tiles are orthogonally adjacent. Manual repair: downgraded MT3 [9,5] greenPotion to redPotion for potion progression. Manual repair: downgraded MT3 [1,1] bluePotion to redPotion for potion progression.

notes: MT0 guard coordinate repaired from [1,2] to [3,3].
Potion progression repaired by downgrading selected high-tier potions to redPotion on MT1-MT3.
